Name: Rashmila Mitra
Description:
- Connected with Adam to refine the UTM tracking logic and understand the current Supabase schema structure for future integration with GA4 and Looker.
- Created a detailed process document titled Oasis: Student Community UTM Tracking & Dashboard Implementation, outlining end-to-end tracking from UTM creation to dashboard reporting.
- Document captured six project phases:  UTM creation, GA4 setup, Supabase schema design, Looker dashboard build, community rollout, and optimization.
- Designed a visual data flow summarizing the journey from UTM-tagged interactions → GA4 → Supabase → Looker → Insights.
- Proposed the Supabase schema skeleton (student_community_analytics) with three key tables:
1. utm_campaigns (campaign metadata)
2. events (engagement and traffic data)
3. conversions (final user actions)

- Documented relationships, indexing recommendations, and next steps for automating GA4 → Supabase data sync and testing ingestion.
- Outlined Looker Studio dashboard structure with tabs for Overview, Engagement & Conversion, UTM Deep Dive, and Real-Time Monitoring.
- Added validation checklist to ensure GA4 data accuracy, event visibility, and dashboard refresh latency ≤ 2 hours.
- Planning revisions to the document draft refine schema and dashboard metrics.

Weekly Sprint Updates
- Attended the weekly sprint call, where updates were shared on browser migration, AI assistant upgrades, AWS optimization, and UTM-based marketing attribution.
- B2C team updates covered Discord and Reddit community engagement progress.
Next steps: implement the Supabase schema, validate GA4 data sync, and prepare the first Looker dashboard prototype.

Name: Rashmila Mitra
Description:
1. Sprint Discussion Notes 
- Attended the sprint meeting to align on updates across engineering, design, product, and sales. Key discussions included progress on the Hubs–Firefox integration, AI agent UI enhancements, website design iterations, and plans for B2C/B2B feature development.
- Shared progress on UTM tracking and early dashboard prototyping, connecting upcoming product features with marketing attribution needs.
- Followed conversations on job seeker tools, alumni engagement strategies, and narrative planning for outreach, noting dependencies relevant to tracking and analytics.

2. Design Team Weekly
- Joined the design standup to review updates to the design system foundation, semantic layers, and Figma workflows.
- Discussed the new design Slack channel and use of Rubble for structured website feedback.
- Provided consolidated feedback on layout structure, boundary rules, and required UI refinements needed pre-launch.
- Observed broader efforts to unify interface visuals across products to ensure that upcoming analytics documentation stays consistent with the evolving design direction.

3. B2C Notes 
- Attended the B2C Oasis discussion covering initial experiments, feature ideas, and early design concepts for the job-hunting experience.
- Shared updates on UTM tracking implementation in Supabase, clarifying how attribution will be linked to user engagement metrics across channels.
- Contributed visual and functional inputs to the Oasis mood board.
- Onboarded new member through the Attribution Analytics charter, UTM logic, and objectives for tracking to support team alignment.
- Reviewed content efforts around blogs, comparison tables, and positioning to stay aligned with broader launch messaging.


4. Independent Work
- Continued onboarding across Supabase, GA4, and Looker, reviewing schemas, existing metrics, and event flows.
- Explored Supabase’s SQL editor, connectors, and schema visualizer to understand how tracking data will eventually sit in the system.
- Reviewed internal documentation and B2B/B2C charters to refine understanding of analytics and attribution requirements and prepared notes for the next sprint.

Name: Rashmila Mitra
Description:
Supabase Schema Design for UTM Tracking
- Created the Supabase schema visualizer by running the SQL DDL script that generated the three core tables: utm_campaigns, events, and user_acquisition.
- Purpose: establish a structured foundation for attribution analytics before real data collection begins.
- PK/FK joins link campaigns → events → user acquisition, enabling traceable attribution paths.
- Documented table purposes, field definitions, relationships, and how Supabase displays schema connections.
- Next steps: clarify data sources and ingestion (GA4, Tag Manager, Tally, beta landing pages), request access from Adam, and study the Early Bird B2C pricing strategy to prepare for future attribution work.

Weekly Meetings Cadence

1. Sprint: The team reviewed progress on engineering, design, marketing, and analytics initiatives. Updates included ongoing work on the Hubs feature, split-screen improvements in Firefox, and continued refinements to the website with feedback gathered through Figma. The marketing team discussed LinkedIn graphics, personalization experiments, and development of the social media calendar. Updates were shared on B2C early-access sign-ups, alumni outreach efforts, and the marketing attribution project. Additional discussions covered video content creation, improvements to the marketing calendar, and outreach strategies. Technical updates highlighted enhancements to the AI assistant and onboarding flows. The meeting concluded with next steps related to testing new product versions.

2. Design Team Weekly: The design standup focused on the marketing calendar, website revisions, and video reviews. The team aligned on upcoming seasonal and brand content. Browser feature videos were evaluated, with feedback around pacing, length, and branding. Collaboration continued in Figma and Notion, with discussions about website alignment, color consistency, and visual polish ahead of go-live. Design support was also coordinated for sales and outreach needs.

3. 1:1 with CTO: The 1:1 centered on integrating UTM tracking into the broader analytics plan and evaluating Mixpanel versus Amplitude for usage insights. Plans were made to phase analytics integration, gather dashboard requirements from stakeholders, and refine website imagery and mood board direction to improve product storytelling.

Name: Abhinav Chandra
Description:
Over the course of approximately 14.5 to 18 hours, I primarily focused on business development and outbound outreach. This included engaging with key alumni from my personal LinkedIn network as well as initiating conversations with target companies in the data center enablement and management space, which I see as a strong opportunity given their stringent compliance requirements and operational complexity.

A significant portion of this effort was dedicated to outreach and discovery with organizations offering data center management systems, including Vertive. Notably, I had a productive conversation with Tim Sokol, Regional Sales Director at Votive, where we discussed their product offering and potential alignment the discussion was highly positive and constructive but not business oriented interest was picked up.

In parallel, I analyzed potential customer personas surfaced through Warmly alerts on Slack, evaluating inbound signals and identifying relevant stakeholders. This included researching decision-makers at Charles Schwab, reviewing organizational structure and verticals, and assessing which roles and functions would be most appropriate for targeted outreach. As part of this effort, I reviewed individual profiles (including Will at Charles Schwab) to better understand internal personas, reporting structures, and potential entry points for engagement.

Name: Rashmila Mitra
Description:
1:1 with Adam -
A lead-tracking and sales-collaboration approach was discussed using data from Mixpanel, Google Analytics, Warmly, forms, and sales research (industry, company size, etc.). Combined data feeds fit and intent scores to rank lead quality, with outreach currently limited to leads who explicitly request demos or contact via forms. This was discussed in the context of a custom local project for visualizing leads, with plans to later export CSVs and configure equivalent logic in HubSpot. A simple funnel to measure content/SEO impact and a framework for sizing impact and costs were also discussed, along with product onboarding and feedback flows.

Impact Sizing Approach Doc: Worked on designing and documenting a bottom-up impact sizing framework for Kahana’s SEO-led blog initiative to quantify its potential contribution to growth and revenue. Defined a unified B2C and B2B funnel, built channel-level traffic and conversion assumptions, and incorporated multi-touch attribution aligned with the PLG HubSpot CRM model. Established incrementality measurement using baseline comparisons and assisted conversion logic to ensure causal impact. Outlined required instrumentation, dashboards, and ownership structure to operationalize tracking. The goal was to create a measurable, capital-efficient inbound growth engine that converts high-intent search traffic into qualified leads, subscriptions, and long-term revenue expansion.

Mixpanel product Analytics - worked on building out the first draft of the drop off funnel dashboard (decided as an action point from the impact sizing doc). Current blockers include sequence of events are mismatched that are being logged in the browser, which might cause discrepancy in numbers reported. Worked towards addressing this issue.

Description: Work during this period centered on getting fully up to speed with the GTM Mixpanel Boards & Reports project. This involved carefully going through the Project Charter to understand the overall objectives, strategic direction, and the scope of what we're building. The User Journey Framework was reviewed in parallel to develop a solid understanding of user behaviors, key touchpoints, and the outcomes the dashboards need to reflect. Time was also dedicated to reading the Duolingo growth article referenced in the charter, which gave useful context on growth loops, retention patterns, and the kinds of behavioral questions that make for high-impact analytics. Throughout the review process, observations and clarifying questions were noted down to help sharpen the user journey thinking and identify the right focus areas for dashboard development. The phase wrapped up with pulling together all key takeaways and aligning them with the team's broader question set, which will feed directly into the next stage of planning. Overall, this groundwork was essential for ensuring the dashboard work ahead is well-informed, focused, and tied closely to the project's goals.

Most of the work so far has been exploratory and rule-definition, considering dummy data to prototype the model. Since Adam emphasized prioritizing browser cohorts in Mixpanel (daily/weekly/monthly active, at‑risk, dead), leveraging Supabase last sign-in/activity and/or Mixpanel events, and creating custom events (e.g., user interaction/login success) to track cohorts reliably, planned to build out reports from existing metrics first.

2) Built the Oasis AI Browser dashboard. Defined key events (signup, request, feedback) and created At-Risk WAU/DAU cohorts. Developed Funnel Reports to measure adoption and session success, and Retention Reports for at-risk cohorts, organizing all metrics onto a centralized Dashboard for team monitoring.

3) Discussed with the Product marketing team regarding immersive nature imagery for Oasis branding; blog pipeline status and reducing upload bottlenecks via cross-training; YouTube content pipeline based on Google Trends; feedback and revisions for brand-awareness animation; progress and goals for Mixpanel dashboards; B2C/B2B strategic narratives; and Product Hunt launch strategy plus internal beta feedback.

4) Tested Oasis AI Browser for beta feedback by internal users, identifying functional limitations and bugs. Submitted comprehensive feedback and NPS survey to support product improvement.

Name: Harika Kurra
Description:
Completed the majority of the onboarding tasks between Friday and Thursday. During this period, I reviewed the company guidelines, joined the internal communication channels, set up required tools, and began familiarizing myself with the workflow and documentation.
I had a check-in meeting with Adam on Tuesday where we discussed the product, my onboarding progress, and any questions I had regarding the process.
I also tested several features in Oasis while using it as my default browser. Some observations during testing included:
Attempted to use split screen with YouTube and Google; the browser opened YouTube along with the Mozilla start page instead of the expected layout.
Tried opening Instagram in the left split screen; the browser repeatedly opened Instagram multiple times instead of loading it once.
Noticed that there does not appear to be an autocorrect feature while typing.
